arguments_for_no: The amendment modifies core payment and offer logic, introducing new transaction types and ledger objects which increases the attack surface and complexity of the protocol.

Changes to OfferCreate behavior (returning tecEXPIRED instead of tesSUCCESS for past expiration times) represent a breaking change in transaction result codes that could affect client-side error handling logic.
arguments_for_yes: The amendment provides a critical privacy and security feature (DepositPreauth) allowing users to preauthorize specific senders, reducing the need for public deposit authorization flags.
It fixes a usability bug where cross-currency payments from an account to itself failed with tecNO_PERMISSION when deposit authorization was enabled, now allowing them to succeed.
It corrects inconsistent behavior in OfferCreate transactions by properly rejecting offers with past expiration times instead of silently succeeding without execution.
The amendment is well-defined with clear transaction types, ledger objects, and RPC commands, indicating a mature and tested implementation.

cited_facts.why_it_matters: Describes the functional benefits of DepositPreauth, including privacy enhancements, fixing self-payment failures, and correcting OfferCreate expiration logic, which are strong arguments for adoption.
Establishes the high stakes of amendment voting, requiring validators to ensure compatibility and stability before activation, supporting a cautious but positive evaluation of well-tested features.
Confirms the amendment was introduced in rippled version 1.1.0, indicating it has been part of the software release cycle and likely underwent standard development and testing processes.

output_json.decision_summary: The DepositPreauth amendment introduces valuable functionality for user privacy and payment flexibility while fixing existing bugs in self-payments and offer creation. The benefits of enhanced user control and corrected protocol behavior outweigh the risks associated with the added complexity, especially given its inclusion in a standard rippled release.
output_json.forbidden_stronger_claim: This amendment guarantees zero security vulnerabilities and perfect compatibility with all existing client software.
missing_evidence: Specific test coverage metrics or audit reports for the new DepositPreauth transaction type.
Data on the prevalence of self-payment failures or past-expiration offer attempts in the current network to quantify the impact of the bug fixes.
